desert.title: Dead zone, no insects, no birds
Glasseye flats
desert.desc: The sand here has fused into near-perfect mirrors. Navigation is disorienting and the heat doubles back on itself.

desert.title: Dried oasis
desert.desc: A cracked basin ringed by dead palms. The water table collapsed years ago. Something still lives in the roots.

desert.title: Functioning oasis
desert.desc: Small, unmarked on any map the party owns. The water is clean. Someone has been here recently.

desert.title: Camelon sacred oasis
desert.desc: A living spring the Camelon call Dhat al-Wadi. A carved stone basin sits at the center. Taking water without leaving water is an offense.

desert.title: Contested oasis
desert.desc: Two factions are camped on opposite sides of the water. Neither will leave. Neither will attack first.

desert.title: Salt flat mine
desert.desc: Abandoned shafts still gaping open. Slabs of grey salt litter the ground. The equipment left behind is worth something to someone.

desert.title: Salt spring
desert.desc: A small seep of slightly toxic brine. Usable in small quantities. Animals avoid it.

desert.title: Salt trade marker
desert.desc: A carved post indicating a price station ahead. The price listed has been crossed out and rewritten three times.

desert.title: Desert glass field
desert.desc: Meteorite-formed glass stretches for a quarter mile. Each piece is unique. The Glass Lord has surveyors who map these fields.

desert.title: Natural glass cave
desert.desc: A small entrance leads into a bubble of fused sand. The walls refract light in uncomfortable directions.

desert.title: Ruined waystation
desert.desc: Walls still standing, no roof. Old fire rings, broken furniture, a door that opens onto nothing.

desert.title: Ivory Lord circuit post
desert.desc: A marker on the Ivory Lord's rotation. The post lists the next waystation, the current toll rate, and a warning not to interfere with the circuit.

desert.title: Road Lord checkpoint ruins
desert.desc: Burned down recently. The toll ledger is ash. Someone was making a point.

desert.title: Partial crypt entrance
desert.desc: Half buried by a recent dune shift. The exposed stone is older than the Scarab Lord titles. No seal on the door.

desert.title: Sealed crypt
desert.desc: A Scarab Lord seal on the door. The wax is intact. The seal belongs to a lord who has not claimed this region.

desert.title: Shallow looted crypt
desert.desc: Already emptied. The traps were not. At least two triggered recently judging by the scorch marks.

desert.title: Deep crypt, unknown sigils
desert.desc: The entrance is sealed with markings no one in the party recognizes. They are not in any script the Ink Lord catalogues.

desert.title: Intact crypt antechamber
desert.desc: The outer room is undisturbed. The inner door is sealed. Something on the other side moves occasionally.

desert.title: Royal tomb, untouched
desert.desc: No evidence of entry. The seals are perfect. The surrounding sand has no footprints, not even animals.

desert.title: Collapsed crypt
desert.desc: The roof came down. Something underneath is still moving.

desert.title: Savra tomb carvings
desert.desc: Surface rock carved with Savra ancestor script. No entrance visible. The carvings describe a king who was buried standing up.

desert.title: Bone Lord marker stone
desert.desc: A single black stone with the Bone Lord's mark. No text. No explanation. Locals know what it means.

desert.title: Hollow Queen dream site
desert.desc: Anyone who sleeps here reports the same dream. A vast dark hall, rows of preserved dead, and a woman who asks them their name.

desert.title: Exposed burial goods
desert.desc: A recent break-in. Gold leaf and oil jars scattered across the sand. Whoever opened the tomb left in a hurry.

desert.title: Coyote story-fire
desert.desc: A ring of old ash and scattered bones from many meals. Coyotes camp here seasonally. The stories they left behind are scratched into the nearby rock.

desert.title: Desert Orc dome
desert.desc: A stone dome structure built from moved rock. Occupied. The leader is whoever won the last fight.

desert.title: Scorpikin territorial marker
desert.desc: Fresh kills arranged in a pattern only Scorpikin read. This land has an owner. They are nearby.

desert.title: First Sting holy site
desert.desc: A flat stone marked with the oldest Scorpikin script. Warriors press a cut palm to it before battle. The stone is always warm.

desert.title: Varuuk shrine
desert.desc: Storm-worn stones arranged in a spiral. Small offerings left recently. No one builds near this spot.

desert.title: Akhemu shrine
desert.desc: A ring of black stones, no roof, no walls. Open to the noon sun. No one speaks near it.

desert.title: Tanawak crossroads
desert.desc: Three paths diverge here. The signs point in different directions and contradict each other. One of them is correct.

desert.title: Spice grove
desert.desc: A clutch of rare spice plants unmarked on any map. The Spice Lord has people who look for exactly this.

desert.title: Shallow gold vein
desert.desc: A storm exposed it. The vein is shallow but real. Three different parties will want to know about this.

desert.title: Camelon water cache
desert.desc: A flat stone concealing a sealed clay pot of water. A Camelon guide mark is scratched nearby. This is someone's emergency.

desert.title: Treasure cache
desert.desc: Old. Partially looted. Something remains at the bottom of the hole, wrapped in leather that has mostly rotted.

desert.title: Wrecked sand skiff
desert.desc: Hull cracked, one runner bent. Cargo compartment possibly intact. No sign of the crew.

desert.title: Downed airship
desert.desc: Recent. The gondola is mostly intact. The crew's fate is unclear. Something picked through the cargo already.

desert.title: Active caravan camp
desert.desc: Mid-journey rest. Tents up, fires going, guards posted. They will trade but not talk about where they're headed.

desert.title: Caravan ambush site
desert.desc: Recent. Blood on the sand, scattered goods, broken wheels. Unclear who won.

desert.title: Sandstorm scar
desert.desc: The landscape was rearranged. A known route no longer exists here. The old map is wrong.

desert.title: Ink Lord survey flag
desert.desc: Fresh. The surveyor is either nearby or dead. The flag marks something the Ink Lord wants mapped.

desert.title: Blood Lord contract post
desert.desc: A small outpost of rough canvas and poles drilled into the sand. Mercenaries can sign up for jobs, bounties, and contracts.

desert.title: Storm Lord observation post
desert.desc: Unmanned. Instruments still running, readings still recording. The Storm Lord reviews these remotely.

desert.title: Star Lord navigation pillar
desert.desc: Ancient stone pillar carved with star positions. Still accurate. The Star Lord did not build this. No one knows who did.

desert.title: Ancient road segment
desert.desc: Paved stone, partially buried. Pre-Scarab Lord. The road goes somewhere that no current map names.

desert.title: Natural spire
desert.desc: A stone column visible for miles. Used as a navigation landmark by everyone. No one controls it but everyone references it.

desert.title: Sand predator hunting ground
desert.desc: Bones everywhere. Picked clean. The kills are fresh enough that whatever did this is nearby.

mountain.title: Narrow mountain pass
mountain.desc: Unguarded. Locally known. Wide enough for a cart if you don't rush it.

mountain.title: Secret Camelon pass
mountain.desc: Not on any map. The Camelon who know it do not share it freely.

mountain.title: Road Lord toll gate
mountain.desc: A solid iron gate across the only viable path. Guards rotate every six days. The rate board is posted clearly.

mountain.title: Fortified watchtower
mountain.desc: Abandoned but intact. The sight lines are excellent. Someone left in a hurry judging by what they left behind.

mountain.title: Heliograph tower
mountain.desc: A working signal mirror mounted at the peak. Unmanned. The code is not publicly known.

mountain.title: Ancient carved stairway
mountain.desc: Cut directly into the cliff face. No railing. Origin unknown. It leads somewhere the current maps do not show.

mountain.title: Ancient carved faces
mountain.desc: Three enormous faces looking down at the pass below. Pre-Scarab Lord. They watch everything that passes.

mountain.title: Gold Lord mine entrance
mountain.desc: Sealed and guarded. The guards rotate weekly and do not make conversation. Unauthorized entry is handled internally.

mountain.title: Copper Lord mining camp
mountain.desc: Active operation, workers present. Heavily organized. Visitors are logged and given a pass number.

mountain.title: Copper Lord foundry outpost
mountain.desc: Armed. Requires a pass to enter. The smell of hot metal is detectable from a mile away.

mountain.title: Disputed mine
mountain.desc: Two Scarab Lord flags planted ten feet apart. A standoff has been ongoing for weeks. Neither side will be the first to swing.

mountain.title: Red Kobari settlement
mountain.desc: Built into a volcanic vent network. Hot, loud, and deeply unwelcoming to outsiders who have not been introduced properly.

mountain.title: Pyre Drakonari nest
mountain.desc: Active. Territorial. The nest is visible from below. The Drakonari are also visible from below.

mountain.title: Ashkind shrine
mountain.desc: A volcanic vent the Red Kobari consider the breath of their god. Prayers are scratched into every surface. Interrupting a ritual here has consequences.

mountain.title: Desert Orc dome
mountain.desc: Stone dome built from moved boulders. The construction is impressive. The inhabitants are not interested in visitors.

mountain.title: Abandoned dome
mountain.desc: Empty. Contents possibly intact. No indication of why it was left.

mountain.title: Savra ancestor temple
mountain.desc: Cut directly into the mountain. The Golden Ancestor markings on the door mean outsiders are not welcome. The Savra inside are polite about this.

mountain.title: Bone Lord deep shaft
mountain.desc: Descends into the tomb network beneath the mountain. The Bone Lord's mark is above the entrance. The shaft has no ladder.

mountain.title: Mountain crypt
mountain.desc: Older than any Scarab Lord title. The stone work predates everything in the current catalogues.

mountain.title: Sealed cliff vault
mountain.desc: Set into the rock face with no markings and no visible mechanism. Someone knows how to open it.

mountain.title: Storm Lord weather station
mountain.desc: Automated instruments recording wind, pressure, and temperature. The readings feed into the Storm Lord's network. Tampering is noticed.

mountain.title: Star Lord summit pillar
mountain.desc: Navigation point for the desert below. The pillar is older than the Star Lord title. They did not build this.

mountain.title: Blood Lord staging camp
mountain.desc: Mercenaries between contracts. Some are waiting for work. Some are waiting for something else.

mountain.title: Ink Lord cartography cache
mountain.desc: A sealed box containing partially updated maps. The surveyor who left it has not returned to collect it.

mountain.title: Gold vein exposure
mountain.desc: Visible on the cliff face. Accessible. Also visible from the road below, which means this will not stay a secret.

mountain.title: Gem deposit
mountain.desc: Semi-precious stones exposed by a recent quake. The seam is shallow but there is more below.

mountain.title: Unregistered copper deposit
mountain.desc: Somebody already knows about this. The signs of recent inspection are visible if you look.

mountain.title: Hidden armoury
mountain.desc: A Copper Lord contract cache. Weapons, tools, and a sealed manifest of what was stored and by whom.

mountain.title: Mountain spring
mountain.desc: Clean water. Contested between two Desert Orc clans. The argument is ongoing and neither side is willing to share.

mountain.title: High-altitude oasis
mountain.desc: Cold spring, small, unmarked. The altitude keeps most travelers away. The water is exceptional.

mountain.title: Underground waterfall
mountain.desc: A river emerges from the rock as a waterfall, crosses forty feet of open air, and vanishes back into the mountain.

mountain.title: Petrified forest
mountain.desc: Stone trees in perfect formation. Ancient. Probably magical in origin. The Ink Lord has been trying to date them for twenty years.

mountain.title: Giant raptor nest
mountain.desc: Eggs present. Parents circling overhead. The nest is between the party and the pass.

mountain.title: Wrecked supply crawler
mountain.desc: Road collapse. The cargo is spread across the slope. Some of it is still usable. Some of it is dangerous.

mountain.title: Crashed airship
mountain.desc: Recent. Half hanging over the cliff edge. The remaining cargo is worth recovering if someone anchors the wreck first.

mountain.title: Kobari den network
mountain.desc: Extensive tunnels. Children present, adults armed. The entrance is well-defended and not obviously marked.

mountain.title: Mosaic Kobari outpost
mountain.desc: More diplomatic than Red Kobari. They trade and negotiate. They also remember every deal ever made with outsiders.

mountain.title: Earthquake fissure
mountain.desc: Fresh. A new cave system partially revealed. What is inside has not been assessed.

mountain.title: Flooded mine shaft
mountain.desc: Something drowned in it recently. The water is dark and still.

mountain.title: Avalanche debris
mountain.desc: Tomb goods mixed with rock. Gold leaf and carved bone scattered across a fifty-foot slide zone.

mountain.title: Bandit camp
mountain.desc: Using decommissioned Gold Lord infrastructure. The equipment is better than typical bandits. Someone funded this.

mountain.title: Territorial dispute
mountain.desc: Pyre Drakonari and Scorpikin in active standoff. Violence is likely. Both sides will interpret an outsider's presence as choosing a side.

mountain.title: Forgotten fort
mountain.desc: Pre-Scarab Lord. Siege weapons still mounted. The mechanisms are seized with rust but the structure is sound.

mountain.title: Ancient road checkpoint
mountain.desc: Pre-Scarab Lord construction. Structurally sound. Someone has been maintaining it without making themselves known.

mountain.title: Pyre Drakonari ritual
mountain.desc: In progress. Dangerous to interrupt. The ashes being scattered belong to someone who made the journey from the east specifically for this.

mountain.title: Gold Lord assay station
mountain.desc: Samples being weighed. The atmosphere is tense. Someone's numbers do not match last month's numbers.

mountain.title: Ivory Lord hunting party
mountain.desc: Tracking something large. They have been out here for nine days and the mood has shifted.

city.title: Caravan staging yard
city.desc: Busy. Multiple Scarab Lords represented. Everyone is pretending not to watch everyone else.

city.title: Waystation tavern
city.desc: Three Scarab Lords have eyes in here. The food is good. The conversation is carefully managed.

city.title: Foxxin brokerage
city.desc: Officially a trading house. Three factions are watching who enters and for how long.

city.title: Corvus messenger hub
city.desc: Birds for hire. Information unofficially available at a separate counter in the back.

city.title: Crow courier roost
city.desc: Renting birds for messages. Discretion costs extra. The birds remember every route they have ever flown.

city.title: Ink Lord reading house
city.desc: Maps for sale openly. Better maps available for a conversation. The best maps require something the Ink Lord wants.

city.title: Ink Lord printing press
city.desc: First edition of a new desert map being distributed today. The information on it is three months old and already being argued about.

city.title: Glass Lord gallery
city.desc: High-end relics on display. A fence operation runs through the back. The distinction between the two is mostly aesthetic.

city.title: Relic scholar
city.desc: Offering authentication services. Also a fence. The authentication is genuine. The provenance documentation is not.

city.title: Relic auction
city.desc: Stolen goods. No questions asked. Three buyers are here on behalf of different Scarab Lords who cannot be seen attending.

city.title: Black market stall
city.desc: Hollow Queen adjacent goods. The seller does not explain what that means. The items are cold to the touch.

city.title: Salt Lord factor house
city.desc: Heavily secured. Tax records inside. The clerks are polite and keep excellent notes on everyone who enters.

city.title: Gold Lord assay office
city.desc: Bribes accepted for better weights. This is an open secret. The Salt Lord disapproves loudly and uses the same service.

city.title: Spice Lord market
city.desc: Priced for nobles. Controlled quantities. The perfume of the place is overwhelming and almost certainly intentional.

city.title: Spice market price war
city.desc: Two brokers shouting in public. The price of something has shifted. The reason behind the shift is more interesting than the argument.

city.title: Ivory Lord trade post
city.desc: Mounted heads on every wall. The inventory leans toward things that should not be for sale.

city.title: Copper Lord parts depot
city.desc: Everything mechanical comes through here. Skiff parts, crawler components, tools. The wait list for some items is eight months.

city.title: Road Lord factor
city.desc: Route passes and toll exemptions for sale. The price depends on who you work for. Some combinations are not offered.

city.title: Storm Lord weather post
city.desc: Public forecasts updated at dawn. The forecast is never wrong. The Storm Lord does not explain how.

city.title: Star Lord navigator school
city.desc: Star charts sold at the door. The full curriculum requires an interview and a deposit the size of a caravan contract.

city.title: Blood Lord recruitment board
city.desc: Contracts posted publicly. The rates are honest. The jobs that pay most are described in deliberately vague terms.

city.title: Bone Lord office
city.desc: Quiet. Few visitors. The ones who enter look calm. The ones who exit look different.

city.title: Bone Lord courier
city.desc: Alone, carrying a sealed case, moving quickly. Nervous in a way that has nothing to do with the streets.

city.title: Caishen temple
city.desc: Funded by the Gold Lord. Small, well-maintained. The priests are aware of the arrangement and have opinions about it.

city.title: Dhat al-Wadi shrine
city.desc: A water offering basin in the middle of the city. Even here, even paved over, the Camelon leave water in it.

city.title: Tanawak shrine
city.desc: Hidden in plain sight. Looks like a junk stall. The items for sale have no obvious purpose.

city.title: Akhemu noon shrine
city.desc: Open air, city center. No one speaks near it. Traffic routes around it without any signs asking them to.

city.title: Foxxin Clever Dead shrine
city.desc: Family specific. Outsiders are not welcome. The family in question has been here for six generations and remembers everything.

city.title: Savra scholar hall
city.desc: Ancient tomb knowledge, carefully guarded. Outsiders can purchase basic reference texts. Everything useful requires an introduction.

city.title: Red Kobari forge district
city.desc: Loud, hot, no outsider discount. The work is exceptional. The wait is long and the Kobari will tell you exactly why it is worth it.

city.title: Camelon caravan guild
city.desc: Hiring today. Guards, guides, cooks, and translators. The contracts are fair. The routes are not disclosed until departure.

city.title: Scorpikin dueling circle
city.desc: Public. Regulated. Bettable. The Scorpikin running it are scrupulously honest about the odds.

city.title: Coyote storytelling circle
city.desc: Outsiders can listen for a coin. The stories are true. The details have been adjusted for the audience.

city.title: Merchant tokens exchange
city.desc: Three currencies in play today. The rates are posted. Two of the rates are accurate.

city.title: Sand skiff track
city.desc: Illegal. Extremely popular. The bets are larger than the prize money. The Copper Lord supplies the replacement parts.

city.title: Desert turtle paddock
city.desc: Three turtles available. Bids open. The seller knows more about these animals than they are disclosing.

city.title: Airship dock
city.desc: One ship. Delayed. The listed reason is weather. The actual reason is being negotiated inside.

city.title: Skiff chop shop
city.desc: Legitimate on the surface. The provenance of some components does not hold up to scrutiny.

city.title: Underground caravan road
city.desc: Below the city. Old. Still used by people who do not want to be seen using the surface roads.

city.title: Public trial
city.desc: A crowd gathered. The charges are political and the outcome appears to be predetermined. The defendant knows this.

city.title: Political assassination
city.desc: Just occurred. The city guard is mobilizing. The assassin is probably still in the crowd.

city.title: Trade dispute
city.desc: Salt Lord and Gold Lord factions, swords out. The street has cleared. Everyone is watching from inside.

city.title: Prison wagon
city.desc: Copper Lord insignia. Moving quickly. The prisoner's identity is not posted. Someone very much wants to know who it is.

city.title: Returned western caravan
city.desc: Just back from the Boiling Reaches. The crew is shaken. They are not talking about what they saw and they are not sleeping.

city.title: Oasis festival
city.desc: All Scarab Lords represented. Temporary peace enforced by mutual agreement. The peace lasts exactly as long as the festival.

city.title: Varuuk storm caller
city.desc: Offering weather manipulation at a price that has not been stated. The negotiation is happening in the open, which is itself unusual.

city.title: Ash Lord sighting
city.desc: A representative from the western reaches has been spotted in the city. This has not happened before. The other Lords are aware.

city.title: Collapsed tunnel
city.desc: A city block has sunk slightly. The hole reveals an older city underneath with different architecture and unknown writing.
